concerns: Hard to find quiet spot — music playing loudly inside and outside constantly
Main dining chicken and fish dishes can be dry and inconsistent
Wine and buffet items ran out mid-cruise — supply issues
Cabins near Martini Bar experience significant ambient noise
highlights: OVERNIGHT IN ISTANBUL — only cruise with 2-day Istanbul stop in this list
Breathtaking entertainment, outstanding dining, stunning Edge-class design
Infinite balcony cabins — innovative glass-to-floor design unique to Edge class
Le Voyage by Daniel Boulud — expanded Michelin-starred chef restaurant

mainDiningHighlights: 4 complimentary main dining rooms: Cyprus, Cosmopolitan, Normandie, Tuscan
Eden Restaurant — 3-level open kitchen with innovative dishes
specialtyRestaurants: Le Voyage by Daniel Boulud (Michelin-star chef)
Le Grand Bistro with Le Petit Chef
foodDetails.menuStyle: Michelin-quality à la carte + 29 dining/bar venues
foodDetails.comparisonNotes: Edge-class flagship with best food of any mainstream ship. Le Voyage by Daniel Boulud is a true fine-dining experience. Complimentary restaurants praised as better than specialty on other lines. Editor rating 4.5/5.

deals.description: The newest Edge-class flagship from Celebrity — 11 nights roundtrip from Rome with an overnight in Istanbul, the only cruise on this list offering two full days in Turkey's cultural capital. Visits Santorini, Kusadasi (Ephesus ruins), Mykonos, and Naples. Premium dining by Michelin-starred Daniel Boulud.
cons: Balcony at $2,487/pp exceeds $2,000 budget — inside cabin ($1,838) fits
11 nights is longer than target 7-9 days
Noise complaints near Martini Bar and pool areas
Wine and buffet supplies ran out mid-cruise on some sailings
pros: Only cruise with OVERNIGHT in Istanbul — 2 full days to explore
CruiseCritic editor 4.5/5 — highest rated ship on this list
Edge-class infinite balcony cabins are stunning
Le Voyage by Daniel Boulud — genuine Michelin-quality dining at sea
Inside cabin at $1,838 fits budget and still gets the Istanbul experience

concerns: Ship too small for 5,000 guests — packed like sardines everywhere
Service level doesn't justify premium price — constant nickel and diming
Impossible to find space — no deck chairs, no buffet tables, no pool spots
highlights: Editor rating 5.0/5 — stunning modern ship with incredible activities
SeaPlex (indoor sports/entertainment complex) is genuinely fun
Includes Kusadasi (Turkey) — Ephesus ancient ruins within budget
Check-in fastest and easiest ever experienced on a cruise

mainDiningHighlights: Two-deck Main Dining Room with LED art walls and crystal chandelier
El Loco Fresh — complimentary pool-deck tacos and burritos
foodDetails.menuStyle: Main dining + 15+ specialty and casual venues
foodDetails.comparisonNotes: Modern ship with impressive dining variety. Giovanni's and Wonderland are standouts. Editor 5.0/5 but user reviews flag overcrowding and cost-cutting. Best entertainment of any ship in this list.

deals.description: Modern Quantum Ultra-class ship — 7 nights roundtrip Rome hitting Santorini, Kusadasi (Turkey), Mykonos, and Naples. Built 2021 with SeaPlex indoor sports complex and North Star observation pod. CruiseCritic editor 5/5.
cons: Overcrowded — 5,000+ guests, hard to find pool/deck space
Nickel-and-diming on extras
Balcony $1,705 leaves tight margin for flights
Buffet quality declining per recent reviews
pros: CruiseCritic editor 5.0/5 — best editorial rating here
Includes Kusadasi (Turkey) — Ephesus ancient ruins
Best onboard activities — SeaPlex, North Star, sky-diving simulator
Built 2021 — newest Royal Caribbean ship in Med

concerns: Ship is older and shows its age despite 2025 Royal Amplified refurb
Windjammer buffet crowded and food quality inconsistent
Pool deck too small for passenger count on sea days
highlights: 2025 refurb brought modern updates — feels fresh and clean
Best value Royal Caribbean ship for Mediterranean — great price point
Excellent Greek Islands itinerary from Venice area

mainDiningHighlights: Main Dining Room — two-deck traditional dining
Windjammer Marketplace — buffet with international stations
foodDetails.comparisonNotes: Standard Royal Caribbean dining — reliable but not exceptional. Refurb added new casual venues. Chops Grille is the standout specialty option.

deals.description: Best value balcony deal — 7 nights roundtrip from Ravenna (Venice area) visiting Santorini, Athens, Mykonos, and Argostoli. Recently refurbished in 2025 with Royal Amplified upgrades. End-of-August sailing means slightly lower prices.
cons: Ship built in 2000 — oldest in the fleet despite 2025 refurb
No Turkey ports on this itinerary
Late August/early September dates may not suit everyone
Windjammer buffet gets crowded and inconsistent
pros: Cheapest balcony at $1,328/pp — most budget-friendly option
2025 Royal Amplified refurb makes it feel modern
Roundtrip Venice area — easy flight from Chisinau
Santorini + Athens + Mykonos — hits the top 3 Greek islands

concerns: Ship is smaller and older — fewer amenities than newer NCL ships
Freestyle dining means long waits at popular restaurants during peak hours
Entertainment options limited compared to larger NCL ships
highlights: 6 ports in 7 nights — incredible port-heavy itinerary from Athens to Venice
Full-size bowling alley — first at sea, unique to this ship
Freestyle Dining means no fixed schedule — eat when and where you want

mainDiningHighlights: Summer Palace — main dining room
Indigo — second main dining
foodDetails.menuStyle: Freestyle Dining — no fixed times, multiple venues
foodDetails.comparisonNotes: Smaller ship means fewer specialty options than Norwegian Getaway, but Cagney's and Le Bistro are consistently praised. Freestyle Dining is a big plus — no rigid schedules.

deals.description: Most ports per night — 6 countries in 7 nights from Athens to Venice. Santorini, Katakolon (Olympia), Corfu, Kotor (Montenegro), Dubrovnik, Koper (Slovenia), and Ravenna. One-way sailing lets you start in Greece and end near Venice. Freestyle Dining with no fixed schedules.
cons: One-way sailing — need separate flights for Athens and Venice
Older ship (2006) — feels dated despite 2025 refurb
Freestyle dining can mean long waits at peak hours
Smaller ship has limited pool and sun deck space
pros: 6 countries in 7 nights — most diverse itinerary available
Athens to Venice route is a dream Mediterranean journey
Freestyle Dining — eat when/where you want, no dress code
Kotor and Dubrovnik are bucket-list destinations
First ship at sea with a full-size bowling alley

concerns: Dining room service reliably bad — missed orders frequently
Ship looks and feels tired — cabin cramped, dated, poorly maintained
Total loss of power at sea — no hot water, non-flushing toilets
highlights: Includes Turkey (Kusadasi/Ephesus) — only cruise in budget with Turkey stop
Smaller guest count of 2,170 — less crowding than mega-ships
Staff amazing, goes above and beyond — Celebrity service standard

mainDiningHighlights: Trellis Restaurant — main dining, reliable quality
Bistro on Five — crepes, paninis, salads (complimentary)
foodDetails.comparisonNotes: Solid mid-range. Main dining reliable but occasionally inconsistent. Tuscan Grille expensive at ~$70/pp. 2024 refit helped. Smaller ship means better dining experience than mega-ships.

deals.description: Best value Turkey option — 7 nights roundtrip Athens with stops in Santorini, Rhodes, Kusadasi (Ephesus ruins in Turkey), Mykonos, and Thessaloniki. Celebrity Infinity was refurbished in 2024 with new suites and public spaces. Smaller ship (2,170 guests) means less crowding.
cons: Ship built 2001 — can feel tired and cramped in standard cabins
Dining room service inconsistent — missed orders are a known issue
Power outage incidents reported on some sailings
Inside cabin at $1,024 might be a better budget choice
pros: Only 7-night cruise with Turkey (Kusadasi) in budget at $1,416 balcony
Smaller ship — 2,170 guests vs 5,000+ on mega-ships
Celebrity service standard — staff goes above and beyond
Rhodes and Thessaloniki are unique ports not on other itineraries
2024 refurb added new public spaces and suites

concerns: Ship is older and shows its age despite refurbishment

highlights: Royal Amplified 2025 refurb — modern feel with updated public spaces

foodDetails.comparisonNotes: 8-night version of the Celebrity Infinity Greece+Turkey itinerary. Extra night adds Thessaloniki. Best food quality in this price range after Celebrity Apex.

deals.description: Extended 8-night sailing for Greece completists — adds Thessaloniki (Greece's second city) to the Turkey route. Roundtrip Athens visiting Rhodes, Kusadasi (Turkey), Mykonos, Thessaloniki, and Santorini. The extra night gives you a full day in Thessaloniki's vibrant food scene.
cons: At $1,674 balcony, leaves only $326 for flights — very tight

pros: Most comprehensive Greece+Turkey itinerary available

8 nights means more relaxed pace with sea days
Roundtrip Athens — simplest flights from Chisinau
Rhodes, Kusadasi, Mykonos, Santorini — all the highlights

deals.description: Most ports of any deal — 8 stops in 7 nights from Venice to Athens. Dubrovnik, Kotor (Montenegro), Corfu, Katakolon (Olympia), Mykonos, and Santorini before ending in Athens. One-way Venice→Athens route is the reverse of the Jul 5 sailing.
cons: Most expensive Norwegian Pearl at $1,719 — peak summer pricing

Reverse direction means less time in Greek islands, more in Croatia

pros: 8 ports in 7 nights — a new destination every day, zero wasted days
